Add a manual override to a reconciliation

Source: R/reconcile_accessors.R
reconcile_override.Rd

Creates an updated reconciliation object with a manual name correction applied.

Usage

reconcile_override(
  x,
  name_x,
  name_y = NULL,
  action = c("accept", "reject", "replace"),
  note = ""
)

Arguments

x

A reconciliation object.

name_x

Character(1). Name from source x.

name_y

Character(1) or NULL. Corrected match in source y. Use NULL to mark the name as deliberately unresolvable.

action

Character(1). One of "accept" (confirm match), "reject" (remove a match), or "replace" (set a new match).

note

Character(1). Justification for the override. Stored in the provenance log.

Value

An updated reconciliation object.

Examples

data(avonet_subset)
data(tree_jetz)
result <- reconcile_tree(avonet_subset, tree_jetz,
                         x_species = "Species1", authority = NULL)
#> ℹ Reconciling 919 data names vs 657 tree tips
#> ℹ Matching 919 x 657 names through 2 stages...
#> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching...
#> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (0 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (657 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (657 matched so far)...
#> ✔ Matched 657/919 data names to tree tips
# Manually assign an unresolved species to a tree tip
unresolved <- reconcile_mapping(result)
unresolved <- unresolved[unresolved$match_type == "unresolved" &
                           unresolved$in_x, ]
if (nrow(unresolved) > 0) {
  result <- reconcile_override(
    result,
    name_x = unresolved$name_x[1],
    name_y = tree_jetz$tip.label[1],
    note = "Manual assignment for demonstration"
  )
}
#> ✔ Override applied: 'Acanthiza cinerea' -> 'Amytornis_barbatus' (accept)
